LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS YEVGENIY LIBOV & MARIYA LIBOV'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED ALTER EGO LIABILITY IN THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. LEEK V COOPER (2011) 194 CAL. APP. 4TH 399, DOES NOT PROVIDE OTHERWISE. DEFENDANT'S FACTUAL OBJECTIONS TO THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE INAPPROPRIATE ON DEMURRER. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED THAT DEFENDANT KNEW OF THE DEFECTS IN THE HOME AND DID NOT DISCLOSE THEM. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE SEVENTH, EIGHTH, AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON FRAUD. PLAINTIFF POINTS TO SPECIFIC MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE BY DEFENDANT TO INDUCE PLAINTIFF TO PURCHASE THE PROPERTY. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRERS TO THE ELEVENTH AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ARE MOOT BASED ON THE COURT'S RULING ON THE MOTION TO STRIKE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS YEVGENIY LIBOV & MARIYA LIBOV'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. THE MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH FIVE OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. IT SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS ALTER EGO LIABILITY AND IS NOT IRRELEVANT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE AND EXEMPLARY DAMAGES IS DENIED. THEY ARE JUSTIFIED WITH REFERENCE TO THE FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. WHILE TECHNICALLY NEW, THEY ARE NOT SO FAR OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THE COURT'S ORDER AS TO JUSTIFY A MOTION TO STRIKE. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE ELEVENTH AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED. THEY ARE ENTIRELY NEW CAUSES OF ACTION, AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT OPPOSED THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST SEEK LEAVE OF COURT TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO ADD NEW CAUSES OF ACTION. (THE COURT WILL SIGN ONE OF THE PROPOSED ORDERS SUBMITTED BY THE PARTIES VIA EMAIL TO THE COURT.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs YEVGENIY LIBOV & MARIYA LIBOV's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied in part and granted in part. The motion to strike paragraph five of the Second Amended Complaint is denied. It sufficiently pleads alter ego liability and is not irrelevant. The motion to strike punitive and exemplary damages is denied. They are justified with reference to the fraud causes of action. The motion to strike the fifth and sixth causes of action is denied. While technically new, they are not so far outside the scope of the Court's order as to justify a motion to strike. The motion to strike the eleventh and twelfth causes of action is granted. They are entirely new causes of action, and Plaintiff has not opposed the motion to strike as to these causes of action. Plaintiff must seek leave of Court to amend the complaint to add new causes of action. (The Court will sign one of the proposed orders submitted by the parties via email to the court.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs YEVGENIY LIBOV & MARIYA LIBOV's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled alter ego liability in the first and second causes of action. Leek v Cooper (2011) 194 Cal. App. 4th 399, does not provide otherwise. Defendant's factual objections to the fifth and sixth causes of action are inappropriate on demurrer. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled that Defendant knew of the defects in the home and did not disclose them. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to support the seventh, eighth, and ninth causes of action based on fraud. Plaintiff points to specific misrepresentations made by Defendant to induce Plaintiff to purchase the property. Defendant's demurrers to the eleventh and twelfth causes of action for emotional distress are moot based on the Court's ruling on the motion to strike. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANTS YEVGENIY LIBOV and MARIYA LIBOV'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The Court grants the motion to strike with 20 days leave to amend to plead more facts to support the claim for punitive damages. The Court denies the remainder of the motion as moot, in light of the rulings on the demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 = (302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANTS YEVGENIY LIBOV and MARIYA LIBOV'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for strict liability and second cause of action for negligent design/construction to plead more facts showing that defendants had a unity of interest with the developer and that an inequitable result will occur if the Court does not pierce the corporate veil. The demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of contract and eighth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant are overruled. Paragraph 11 refers to the written purchase agreement, which is incorporated into the cause of action for breach of contract. Paragraph 14 sufficiently alleges facts to implicate the discovery rule. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for negligence is overruled. Plaintiff pleads a cause of action for negligence and whether plaintiff can recover emotional distress damages should have been raised by a motion to strike and/or can be raised by a motion in limine. The Court sustains the demurrer to the fifth cause of action for plaintiff to plead facts showing that the property is a common interest development subject to CC 1368. The Court sustains the demurrer to the sixth cause of action because plaintiff needs to plead more facts to support the intentional misrepresentation cause of action and plaintiff should separate the intentional misrepresentation and concealment causes of action in compliance with CRC 2.112. The Court sustains the demurrer to the seventh cause of action for negligent misrepresentation to plead more facts to support the claim. The Court sustains the demurrer to the ninth cause of action for rescission because the fraud claims are lacking, as set forth above. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
